title: galium subvillosum var. subvillosum
intro: Galium subvillosum var. subvillosum, also known as lesser bedstraw, is a perennial herbaceous plant in the bedstraw family (Galium) that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe, western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties. Lesser bedstraw has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing blood pressure, treating fever, and alleviating symptoms of urinary tract infections. The plant can be used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
